layman_headline: Crypto markets are bleeding — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The entire crypto market is in a fear-driven selloff, with Bitcoin dropping below $62K and almost every altcoin posting significant losses. BTC dominance is high at 55.9%, meaning money is leaving altcoins and flowing into Bitcoin or exiting crypto entirely. In this environment, buying altcoins is like catching a falling knife — they tend to drop harder than BTC during risk-off periods. The Fear & Greed index at 28 is improving slightly from last week's 24.6, but hasn't reached the extreme fear levels (below 20) that historically mark reliable bottoms.
layman_action: Do not open any new positions right now. Wait for Bitcoin to find a clear floor and for fear to either spike to extreme levels (signaling capitulation) or for a confirmed reversal pattern. Cash is a position — protecting capital in this environment is the smart move.
expert_reasoning: None of the swing candidates present a compelling risk/reward in the current environment. GRASS is down 34.5% in 7 days but has a bearish engulfing candle and -0.90 chart bias. LIT has RSI at 72 (overbought) making it a fade candidate, not a buy. BONK has the worst chart bias at -1.40 with pure selling conviction. VELVET already ran +23% and is extended. ETHFI has the best chart bias (+0.60) but risk/reward to nearest resistance is only 0.11x — terrible. MON is the most interesting with RSI 41, bullish marubozu, and positive 5-day momentum, but it's still below all MAs in a downtrend with BTC dominance crushing alts. The smart play is to wait for BTC to stabilize and for these candidates to either form confirmed bottoms or reach deeper oversold levels.
risk_factors: BTC could break below $60K triggering cascading alt liquidations and another 15-20% alt drawdown
Geopolitical escalation around Hormuz Strait could trigger broader risk-asset selloff
BTC dominance at 55.9% and rising means altcoin capital outflows may accelerate further
watch_list: Bitcoin price action around $60K-$62K support zone — a breakdown here changes everything
Fear & Greed index — watch for a spike to extreme fear (sub-15) which historically marks capitulation bottoms
MON price behavior near $0.0185 support — if it holds with volume, that becomes a high-conviction entry
market_context: Broad crypto selloff with BTC down 3.8% weekly to $61.9K, Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear), and BTC dominance at 55.9% crushing altcoins. Geopolitical risks around the Hormuz Strait are adding pressure to all risk assets.
context_banner: Market-wide fear-driven selloff with no confirmed bottoms — capital preservation takes priority over opportunity hunting.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) with improving trend from 24.6 weekly average — not yet at extreme fear capitulation levels that signal reliable bottoms.
Bitcoin panic-selling may be ending as sellers' profit margins disappear, suggesting we're approaching but haven't confirmed a bottom.
Bitcoin threatens $62K amid risk-asset rout as Trump says US will 'run' the Hormuz Strait — geopolitical uncertainty adding downward pressure.
Best candidates (MON at 41, BONK at 40) are approaching but not yet in oversold territory below 30 where bounce probability increases significantly.
All candidates are below key moving averages with bearish structure; only ETHFI and LIT show moderately bullish chart biases, but risk/reward ratios are poor (0.11x and 1.0x respectively).
